summary: Full testing of 6 new features: Zkušenosti komunity stories section, Q&A reply permissions, NewsPage edit, SupportPage edit, lawyer green badge, admin section settings. Found and fixed 4 critical bugs: 2 JSX syntax errors (NewsPage/SupportPage), edit dialog rendering bug in NewsPage, and missing CommunityPage import in App.js. All 19 backend tests pass. All frontend features verified working after fixes.

bugs_fixed_by_testing_agent.issue: JSX syntax error: closing fragment tag `</>;` missing `)` to close `return (` statement
Same JSX syntax error: `</>;` missing `)` to close `return (`

Edit dialog (Dialog component) was defined in MAIN return but triggered from DETAIL VIEW (early return). When clicking 'Upravit článek' in detail view, the dialog was never rendered because early return exits before main return's Dialog.
bugs_fixed_by_testing_agent.fix: Duplicated the Dialog into the early return (detail view) block so it renders when admin clicks edit from detail view

bugs_fixed_by_testing_agent.issue: CommunityPage was used in Route but not imported - caused compile error

test_results.T1_navigation_zkusenosti: PASS - Nav link 'Zkušenosti komunity' found at /zkusenosti
test_results.T3_sdilet_pribeh_button: PASS - 'Sdílet příběh' button visible for logged-in user
test_results.T5_reply_to_story: PASS - Reply submitted to story, answer input found by data-testid
test_results.T6_news_upravit_clanek_btn: PASS - Admin sees 'Upravit článek' button (Pencil) on news detail
test_results.T7_news_edit_dialog_prefilled: PASS - Edit dialog opens with title pre-filled: 'bylo to tezke'
test_results.T8_support_pencil_buttons: PASS - Admin sees 21 Pencil edit buttons on service cards
test_results.T9_support_edit_dialog: PASS - Edit dialog opens with offer pre-filled: 'Hledam kamarady'
test_results.T10_lawyer_green_badge: PASS - 'Ověřený právník' shown in GREEN (text-emerald-700) in Právní poradna
test_results.T11_specialist_teal_badge: PASS - 'Ověřený odborník' shown in TEAL in Specialists Q&A

critical_code_review_comments: NewsPage.js and SupportPage.js had JSX syntax errors (missing `)` after fragment close tag) - both fixed
NewsPage.js: Edit dialog component was architecturally placed in wrong scope (main return vs detail view early return) - fixed by duplicating Dialog into detail view
App.js: CommunityPage imported but not added to imports - fixed
Consider moving edit Dialogs to separate components to avoid the early-return pattern pitfall
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_iter52_* users/stories/services/questions for testing, cleaned up after each test
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 new features fully working. Iteration 52 fixed 4 critical bugs (2 JSX syntax errors in NewsPage/SupportPage, edit dialog rendering bug in NewsPage detail view, missing CommunityPage import in App.js). Backend 19/19 tests passing. Frontend all features verified. The stories section (/zkusenosti) has QnASection with allowAllReplies=true, legalContext=false. LegalPage passes legalContext=true showing green lawyer badge. Regular users have role='user' and cannot answer legal questions (403) but CAN answer stories. Admin can edit any news and any service from their respective pages.

rca_of_issues.syntax_errors: Both NewsPage.js and SupportPage.js were modified to add edit dialogs. The Dialog JSX was placed at the end of the component return, but the closing fragment tag was written as `</>;` instead of `</>` + `);`. The `;` terminated JavaScript parsing without closing the parenthesized return expression `return (`, causing a SyntaxError in Babel.
rca_of_issues.edit_dialog_not_rendering: NewsPage.js uses an early-return pattern: `if (selected) return (detail view)`. The edit Dialog component was placed at the bottom of the MAIN return. When user is in detail view (selected is set), the early return exits before reaching the main return, so the Dialog never mounts. Fix: Dialog was duplicated into the early return fragment so it's always present in detail view.
rca_of_issues.community_page_import: App.js routes use <CommunityPage /> but the import statement was never added. This caused a ReferenceError at compile time preventing the entire app from loading.
